creator: matthias.andree@gmx.de
text:
Am 15.01.2014 16:57, schrieb Heino Tiedemann:

> the installer ist looking in
> /usr/ports/audio/clementine-player/work/stage/usr/local/share/clementine/projectm-presets/


Note the "work/stage" part followed by $PREFIX - this is crucial.


> but the .milk file arent there, they are in 
> /work/clementine-1.2.1/3rdparty/libprojectm/presets/

That's not where anything is allowed to be packaged from, and
"libprojectm" is not where pkg-plist would have the package building look.

> Why the installer is looking in the "wrong" directory?

The question is: why do the files not get installed into the right
directory under work/stage for you, while they are for sbruno and me?

And note that staging does not make a difference, from your no_stage:

...
> -- Set runtime path of "/usr/local/bin/clementine-tagreader" to "/usr/local/lib:/usr/local/lib/qt4"
> ===>   Registering installation for clementine-player-1.2.1_5
> pkg-static: lstat(/usr/local/share/clementine/projectm-presets/Geiss - Pistons.milk): No such file or directory
> pkg-static: lstat(/usr/local/share/clementine/projectm-presets/Rovastar - The Shroomery.milk): No such file or directory
> pkg-static: lstat(/usr/local/share/clementine/projectm-presets/Idiot - MOTIVATION!.milk): No such file or directory
...

So I'd say we will rule out staging.

The issue is only why we get projectm stuff and you do not.

The other issue is that with staging the mismatch between what got
installed and what's listed in pkg-plist becomes fatal, while without
staging the error is ignored.
